page: Microsoft Azure NV24s_v2 is an NVSv2 family virtual machine configured with 24 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an Intel processor. The system runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a substantial memory allocation of 448.0 GB, translating to 18.67 GB of RAM per core, which positions it as a highly memory-dense resource.

This server type includes two bundled GPUs and 3019 GB of local storage. It is also Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance disk configurations. The combination of dedicated CPU cores, dual GPUs, and a large local storage capacity supports data-intensive operations without relying solely on external network-attached storage.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the NV24s_v2 offers high resource density by combining significant memory capacity, local storage, and dual GPU accelerators on a single virtual machine. This configuration is suitable for workloads requiring parallel processing and large memory footprints, such as GPU-accelerated databases, deep learning inference, visualization, and complex data science jobs.
description: Microsoft Azure NV24s_v2 is an x86_64 virtual machine within the NVSv2 family, powered by an Intel processor and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs and a substantial memory capacity of 448.0 GB, providing 18.67 GB of RAM per core. The hardware configuration is equipped with two GPUs and 3019 GB of local storage, and it supports Premium Storage. This high resource density makes the server qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require simultaneous GPU acceleration, large memory footprints, and high-capacity local storage. The NV24s_v2 is suited for memory-intensive and GPU-accelerated applications, including data science tasks, visualization, and database workloads.

tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual machine featuring high memory density, dedicated Intel processing cores, and substantial local storage capacity.

448.0 GB of RAM providing 18.67 GB per core
Two integrated GPUs for accelerated parallel processing

Designed for GPU-accelerated databases and data science workloads
